title: Inverse Trigonometric Functions

sections.title: Introduction
concepts: The inverse of a function f, denoted by fвҒ»В№, exists if f is one-one and onto
Trigonometric functions are not one-one and onto over their natural domains and ranges, hence their inverses do not exist without restricting domains
By restricting domains and ranges of trigonometric functions, we ensure existence of their inverses
Inverse trigonometric functions play an important role in calculus for defining many integrals
The concepts of inverse trigonometric functions are also used in science and engineering
key_points: If f : X вҶ’ Y such that f(x) = y is one-one and onto, then we can define a unique function g : Y вҶ’ X such that g(y) = x, where x вҲҲ X and y = f(x), y вҲҲ Y
The function g is called the inverse of f and is denoted by fвҒ»В№
Domain of g = Range of f, and Range of g = Domain of f

sections.title: Basic Concepts
concepts: Restricting domains of trigonometric functions to obtain one-one and onto functions
Principal value branch of inverse trigonometric functions
Graphs of inverse trigonometric functions obtained by reflecting graphs of trigonometric functions along y = x

definitions.definition: The branch of an inverse trigonometric function with a specific restricted range that is conventionally chosen as the standard. For sinвҒ»В№, the principal value branch has range [вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2].
The value of an inverse trigonometric function which lies in the range of the principal branch is called the principal value of that inverse trigonometric function.
The inverse of sine function with domain [вҲ’1, 1] and range [вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2]. If sin y = x, then y = sinвҒ»В№ x.
The inverse of cosine function with domain [вҲ’1, 1] and range [0, ПҖ]. If cos y = x, then y = cosвҒ»В№ x.
The inverse of cosecant function with domain R вҲ’ (вҲ’1, 1) and range [вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2] вҲ’ {0}.
The inverse of secant function with domain R вҲ’ (вҲ’1, 1) and range [0, ПҖ] вҲ’ {ПҖ/2}.
The inverse of tangent function with domain R and range (вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2).
The inverse of cotangent function with domain R and range (0, ПҖ).

formulas.name: Domain and Range of cosecвҒ»В№
formulas.notes: Principal value branch. Domain is |x| вүҘ 1, i.e., x вүӨ вҲ’1 or x вүҘ 1

formulas.name: Domain and Range of secвҒ»В№
formulas.notes: Principal value branch. Domain is |x| вүҘ 1, i.e., x вүӨ вҲ’1 or x вүҘ 1

formulas.name: Sine inverse-forward composition
formulas.notes: Composition of function with its inverse

key_points: sinвҒ»В№ x should not be confused with (sin x)вҒ»В№. In fact (sin x)вҒ»В№ = 1/sin x, and similarly for other trigonometric functions
Whenever no branch of an inverse trigonometric function is mentioned, we mean the principal value branch of that function
The graph of y = sinвҒ»В№ x can be obtained from the graph of y = sin x by interchanging x and y axes
The graph of an inverse function is a mirror image (reflection) of the corresponding graph of the original function along the line y = x
Sine function restricted to [вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2] is one-one and onto with range [вҲ’1, 1]
Cosine function restricted to [0, ПҖ] is one-one and onto with range [вҲ’1, 1]
Tangent function restricted to (вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2) is one-one and onto with range R
Cotangent function restricted to (0, ПҖ) is one-one and onto with range R
Cosecant function restricted to [вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2] вҲ’ {0} is one-one and onto with range R вҲ’ (вҲ’1, 1)
Secant function restricted to [0, ПҖ] вҲ’ {ПҖ/2} is one-one and onto with range R вҲ’ (вҲ’1, 1)

sections.title: Properties of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts: Properties valid within the principal value branches of inverse trigonometric functions
Some results may not be valid for all values of the domains of inverse trigonometric functions
Simplification of expressions using inverse trigonometric identities

formulas.name: Cancellation property (sin)
formulas.formula: \sin(\sin^{-1} x) = x, \; x \in [-1,1] \text{ and } \sin^{-1}(\sin x) = x, \; x \in \left[-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]
formulas.notes: Similar results hold for other trigonometric functions for suitable values of domain

formulas.name: Double angle formula for sinвҒ»В№
formulas.notes: Derived by substituting x = sin Оё

formulas.name: Double angle formula for cosвҒ»В№
formulas.notes: Derived by substituting x = cos Оё

formulas.name: Simplification of cotвҒ»В№(1/вҲҡ(xВІ вҲ’ 1))
formulas.notes: Derived by substituting x = sec Оё

key_points: These properties are valid within the principal value branches of the corresponding inverse trigonometric functions and wherever they are defined
Some results may be valid only for some values of x for which inverse trigonometric functions are defined
When sinвҒ»В№(sin x) is evaluated and x is not in [вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2], use trigonometric identities to bring the argument into the principal range

summary: The domains and ranges (principal value branches) of inverse trigonometric functions are: sinвҒ»В№: [вҲ’1, 1] вҶ’ [вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2]; cosвҒ»В№: [вҲ’1, 1] вҶ’ [0, ПҖ]; cosecвҒ»В№: R вҲ’ (вҲ’1, 1) вҶ’ [вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2] вҲ’ {0}; secвҒ»В№: R вҲ’ (вҲ’1, 1) вҶ’ [0, ПҖ] вҲ’ {ПҖ/2}; tanвҒ»В№: R вҶ’ (вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2); cotвҒ»В№: R вҶ’ (0, ПҖ)
sinвҒ»В№ x should not be confused with (sin x)вҒ»В№. In fact (sin x)вҒ»В№ = 1/sin x, and similarly for other trigonometric functions
The value of an inverse trigonometric function which lies in the range of its principal value branch is called the principal value of that inverse trigonometric function
For suitable values of domain: y = sinвҒ»В№ x implies x = sin y
For suitable values of domain: sin(sinвҒ»В№ x) = x
For suitable values of domain: sinвҒ»В№(sin x) = x

If sinвҒ»В№ x = y, then
(A) 0 вүӨ y вүӨ ПҖ
(B) вҲ’ПҖ/2 вүӨ y вүӨ ПҖ/2
(C) 0 < y < ПҖ
(D) вҲ’ПҖ/2 < y < ПҖ/2

tanвҒ»В№ вҲҡ3 вҲ’ secвҒ»В№(вҲ’2) is equal to
(A) ПҖ
(B) вҲ’ПҖ/3
(C) ПҖ/3
(D) 2ПҖ/3
Prove that 3 sinвҒ»В№ x = sinвҒ»В№(3x вҲ’ 4xВі), x вҲҲ [вҲ’1/2, 1/2]
questions.answer: Hint: Substitute x = sin Оё, then use sin 3Оё = 3 sin Оё вҲ’ 4 sinВі Оё

questions.question: Prove that 3 cosвҒ»В№ x = cosвҒ»В№(4xВі вҲ’ 3x), x вҲҲ [1/2, 1]
questions.answer: Hint: Substitute x = cos Оё, then use cos 3Оё = 4 cosВі Оё вҲ’ 3 cos Оё

questions.question: Write tanвҒ»В№(вҲҡ(1 + xВІ) вҲ’ 1)/x, x вү  0, in the simplest form

Write tanвҒ»В№((cos x вҲ’ sin x)/(cos x + sin x)), вҲ’ПҖ/4 < x < 3ПҖ/4, in the simplest form

Find the value of tanвҒ»В№[2 cos(2 sinвҒ»В№(1/2))]
Find the value of tan(1/2)[sinвҒ»В№(2x/(1 + xВІ)) + cosвҒ»В№((1 вҲ’ yВІ)/(1 + yВІ))], |x| < 1, y > 0 and xy < 1
Find the value of sinвҒ»В№(sin 2ПҖ/3)
Find the value of tanвҒ»В№(tan 3ПҖ/4)
Find the value of tan(sinвҒ»В№(3/5) + cotвҒ»В№(3/2))

(C) 0

Find the value of tanвҒ»В№(tan 7ПҖ/6)
Prove that 2 sinвҒ»В№(3/5) = tanвҒ»В№(24/7)
questions.answer: Hint: Let sinвҒ»В№(3/5) = Оё, then sin Оё = 3/5, cos Оё = 4/5. Use sin 2Оё = 2 sin Оё cos Оё and tan 2Оё = sin 2Оё / cos 2Оё to get tan 2Оё = 24/7

questions.question: Prove that sinвҒ»В№(8/17) + sinвҒ»В№(3/5) = tanвҒ»В№(77/36)
questions.answer: Hint: Let sinвҒ»В№(8/17) = Оұ, sinвҒ»В№(3/5) = ОІ. Find tan Оұ = 8/15, tan ОІ = 3/4. Use tan(Оұ + ОІ) = (tan Оұ + tan ОІ)/(1 вҲ’ tan Оұ tan ОІ) = 77/36

questions.question: Prove that cosвҒ»В№(4/5) + cosвҒ»В№(12/13) = cosвҒ»В№(33/65)
questions.answer: Hint: Let cosвҒ»В№(4/5) = Оұ, cosвҒ»В№(12/13) = ОІ. Use cos(Оұ + ОІ) = cos Оұ cos ОІ вҲ’ sin Оұ sin ОІ = (4/5)(12/13) вҲ’ (3/5)(5/13) = 33/65

questions.question: Prove that cosвҒ»В№(12/13) + sinвҒ»В№(3/5) = sinвҒ»В№(56/65)
questions.answer: Hint: Let cosвҒ»В№(12/13) = Оұ, sinвҒ»В№(3/5) = ОІ. Use sin(Оұ + ОІ) = sin Оұ cos ОІ + cos Оұ sin ОІ = (5/13)(4/5) + (12/13)(3/5) = 56/65

questions.question: Prove that tanвҒ»В№(63/16) = sinвҒ»В№(5/13) + cosвҒ»В№(3/5)
questions.answer: Hint: Let sinвҒ»В№(5/13) = Оұ, cosвҒ»В№(3/5) = ОІ. Find tan Оұ = 5/12, tan ОІ = 4/3. Use tan(Оұ + ОІ) = (5/12 + 4/3)/(1 вҲ’ 5/12 . 4/3) = 63/16

questions.question: Prove that tanвҒ»В№(вҲҡx) = (1/2) cosвҒ»В№((1 вҲ’ x)/(1 + x)), x вҲҲ [0, 1]
questions.answer: Hint: Put x = tanВІ Оё, then вҲҡx = tan Оё and (1 вҲ’ x)/(1 + x) = cos 2Оё

questions.question: Prove that cotвҒ»В№((вҲҡ(1 + sin x) + вҲҡ(1 вҲ’ sin x))/(вҲҡ(1 + sin x) вҲ’ вҲҡ(1 вҲ’ sin x))) = x/2, x вҲҲ (0, ПҖ/4)
questions.answer: Hint: Use half-angle identities: вҲҡ(1 + sin x) = cos(x/2) + sin(x/2) and вҲҡ(1 вҲ’ sin x) = cos(x/2) вҲ’ sin(x/2) for x in (0, pi/4), then simplify to get cotвҒ»В№(cot(x/2)) = x/2

questions.question: Prove that tanвҒ»В№((вҲҡ(1 + x) вҲ’ вҲҡ(1 вҲ’ x))/(вҲҡ(1 + x) + вҲҡ(1 вҲ’ x))) = ПҖ/4 вҲ’ (1/2) cosвҒ»В№ x, вҲ’1/вҲҡ2 вүӨ x вүӨ 1 [Hint: Put x = cos 2Оё]
questions.answer: Hint: Put x = cos 2Оё, then вҲҡ(1+x) = вҲҡ2 cos Оё, вҲҡ(1вҲ’x) = вҲҡ2 sin Оё. Expression becomes tanвҒ»В№((cos Оё вҲ’ sin Оё)/(cos Оё + sin Оё)) = tanвҒ»В№(tan(pi/4 вҲ’ Оё)) = pi/4 вҲ’ Оё = pi/4 вҲ’ (1/2) cosвҒ»В№ x

questions.question: Solve: 2 tanвҒ»В№(cos x) = tanвҒ»В№(2 cosec x)
Solve: tanвҒ»В№((1 вҲ’ x)/(1 + x)) = (1/2) tanвҒ»В№ x, (x > 0)

(A) x/вҲҡ(1 вҲ’ xВІ)
(B) 1/вҲҡ(1 вҲ’ xВІ)
(C) 1/вҲҡ(1 + xВІ)
(D) x/вҲҡ(1 + xВІ)

(B) 1, 1/2
(C) 0
(D) 1/2
